name: Endive with Tomato Rosettes
recipeInstructions:
1. Separate the leaves from the heads of Belgian endive, using about six leaves per serving. Reserve any small leaves and centers for salad another day.
2. Rinse the leaves under cold running water and pat dry with paper towels.
3. In a small bowl, beat the cream cheese until smooth. Gradually add milk while continuing to beat until well blended.
4. Stir in the chopped dried tomatoes.
5. Fill a decorating bag fitted with a large rosette tip about one-third full of the cream-cheese mixture.
6. Place a basil leaf or watercress sprig on each endive leaf.
7. Pipe some filling onto the basil at the wide end of each endive leaf.
8. Arrange the leaves on a platter and garnish with flower.
9. Cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.
